assertion_results.text: Output subscribes to kind:24133 events filtered by p-tag matching the signer pubkey
assertion_results.evidence: In relay.ts: const filter = { kinds: [24133], "#p": [subscribeToPubkey], since: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) - 60 }; — subscribeToPubkey is the signer pubkey passed from bunker.ts via this.relayPool.connectAll(this.relays, this.signerPubkey)

assertion_results.text: Output uses NIP-44 (not NIP-04) for decrypting request content and encrypting response content
assertion_results.evidence: In nip44.ts: full NIP-44 v2 implementation with getConversationKey, nip44Encrypt, nip44Decrypt. In bunker.ts: decryptNIP44(event.content, this.signerPrivateKey, clientPubkey) for decryption and encryptNIP44(responseJson, this.signerPrivateKey, clientPubkey) for encryption. NIP-04 is explicitly rejected: 'Method nip04_encrypt/nip04_decrypt is not supported by this bunker.'

assertion_results.text: Output implements a connect handler that validates secrets and stores client sessions with permissions
assertion_results.evidence: handleConnect() validates secret: if (providedSecret !== this.secret) return error; if (this.consumedSecrets.has(providedSecret)) return error 'Secret has already been used'; this.consumedSecrets.add(providedSecret); Negotiates permissions: const grantedPerms = negotiatePermissions(requestedPerms, DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS); Creates session: this.sessions.set(clientPubkey, session);

assertion_results.text: Output implements get_public_key that returns the user pubkey (not the signer transport pubkey)
assertion_results.evidence: handleGetPublicKey(): returns { id: requestId, result: this.userPubkey } — with comment: 'Returns the user's public key (not the signer transport key).'

assertion_results.text: Output implements sign_event that signs with the user's private key
assertion_results.evidence: handleSignEvent(): const signed = signEvent(this.userPrivateKey, unsignedEvent); — uses userPrivateKey, not signerPrivateKey

assertion_results.text: Output enforces permission checking — rejects sign_event requests for kinds other than 0 and 1
assertion_results.evidence: In permissions.ts: isAllowed() checks sign_event kind against permissions. DEFAULT_PERMISSIONS = [{ method: 'sign_event', kind: 0 }, { method: 'sign_event', kind: 1 }, ...]. handleSignEvent checks: if (!isAllowed('sign_event', session.permissions, unsignedEvent.kind)) return error 'Permission denied: sign_event for kind:X is not allowed.'

assertion_results.text: Output generates a bunker:// URI containing the signer pubkey, relay URLs, and optional secret
assertion_results.evidence: getBunkerURI(): params.append('relay', relay); if (this.secret) { params.set('secret', this.secret); } return `bunker://${this.signerPubkey}?${params.toString()}`;

assertion_results.text: Output matches request IDs in responses to enable proper request-response correlation
assertion_results.evidence: All response objects include the request id: { id: requestId, result: 'ack' }, { id: requestId, result: this.userPubkey }, { id: requestId, result: JSON.stringify(signed) }, etc. NIP46Response type requires id field.
